mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a >0.2pp unemployment jump.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of observed cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPI inflation exceeds 0.4% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity and corporate profit momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day SPX forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its long-term trend signals supply-side capacity utilization and inventory restocking, which precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 55% of periods where industrial production crosses above its 12-month moving average.
